card.intuition: The practice of splitting your JS into smaller chunks that can be loaded on demand, ensuring users only download the code they need for the page they are currently visiting.
card.analogy: A library: instead of giving the reader every single book in the building when they walk in the door (One Big Bundle), you give them a catalog. They only go to the shelf to pick up a book when they actually want to read it (Lazy Loading).
when_to_use: Loading heavy libraries (like a 3D viewer) only when requested
Improving the 'Time to Interactive' on slow devices
gotchas: You should almost always wrap lazily-loaded components in a `<Suspense>` boundary to handle the loading state
Splitting too much can actually hurt performance due to 'Cascading Waterfall' requests (A loads B, then B loads C)

exercise.prompt: Identify the two React utilities used together for code splitting.
exercise.test_code:
def check(l, i): return l.lower() == "lazy" and i.lower() == "import"
assert check("lazy", "import")
print("PASS")
